title: secale montanum subsp. chaldicum
intro: Secale montanum subsp. chaldicum, also known as wild rye or mountain rye, is a subspecies of the rye plant, Secale montanum. It is a type of grass that grows in mountainous regions and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant contains bioactive compounds such as saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Wild rye can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, colds, and respiratory infections.
